articles.title: The £12,000 Phone Bill Audit That Every Finance Director Should Read
articles.body:
A finance director at a 90-person accounting firm asked me to review their telecom invoices before budget season. She expected to find maybe £500 in savings. We found £12,000 per year.

Here is the line-by-line breakdown — and how to find the same waste in your own bills.

## The Audit

I requested 6 months of invoices from their two telecom providers. Total monthly spend: £4,200.

| Line Item | Monthly | Annual | Verdict |
|-----------|---------|--------|---------|
| 2 PRI circuits (46 channels) | £1,420 | £17,040 | **WASTE** — peak usage: 12 concurrent calls |
| Maintenance contract | £380 | £4,560 | **OVERPAYING** — 3x market rate |
| Call recording (90 users) | £540 | £6,480 | **SHOULD BE FREE** — included with modern VoIP |
| Voicemail (90 users) | £270 | £3,240 | **SHOULD BE FREE** |
| Auto-attendant | £65 | £780 | **SHOULD BE FREE** |
| Conference bridge | £95 | £1,140 | **SHOULD BE FREE** |
| Long distance | £280 | £3,360 | **SHOULD BE FREE** — unlimited with VoIP |
| IT contractor (phones) | £350 | £4,200 | **REDUCIBLE** — cloud VoIP needs 80% less admin |
| **Total** | **£3,400** | **£40,800** | |

## What We Changed

| Item | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| Phone system | PRI + PBX | Cloud VoIP |
| Monthly cost | £4,200 | £2,160 |
| Features extra | £970/month | £0 (all included) |
| IT admin | 12 hrs/month | 2 hrs/month |

New monthly cost: 90 users × £24/user = £2,160. Everything included.

Monthly savings: £4,200 - £2,160 = **£2,040**
Annual savings: **£24,480**

But we only claimed £12,000 because the remaining savings depended on cancelling the PRI early termination — which cost £8,400. Net year-one savings after ETF: £16,080. Year two onwards: £24,480.

## How to Audit Your Own Bill in 15 Minutes

**Step 1 (3 minutes):** Get your last phone invoice. Find the total.

**Step 2 (3 minutes):** Count phone users. Divide total by users = per-user cost.

| Per-User Cost | Assessment |
|--------------|------------|
| Under £20 | You are already on VoIP or a great deal |
| £20-35 | Room for optimisation |
| £35-50 | Significantly overpaying |
| Over £50 | Urgently need to switch |

**Step 3 (3 minutes):** Look for line items that should be free:
- Call recording
- Voicemail
- Auto-attendant
- Conference calling
- Long distance
- Mobile app

If ANY of these appear as separate charges, you are on an outdated plan.

**Step 4 (3 minutes):** Count your phone lines/channels. Compare to your peak concurrent calls (ask your IT team or check your PBX CDR). If you have more than 1.5× your peak, you are paying for ghost lines.

**Step 5 (3 minutes):** Get a comparison quote. [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) offers free bill analysis — email your invoices and get a detailed savings report within 48 hours. No obligation, no sales pressure.

The average company saves 40-70% when they run this audit. The £12,000 we found was actually conservative — most companies waste more.

articles.title: What Happens in the First 72 Hours After Switching to DialPhone
articles.body:
We track every migration meticulously. Here is the actual timeline of what happens when a business switches to [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com), based on data from our last 200 deployments.

## Hour 0-1: Account Setup

**What happens:** Your dedicated migration specialist creates your account, sets up your organisational structure (departments, locations), and configures your phone number plan.

**What you do:** Nothing. We handle this based on the information you provided during sign-up.

**Average time:** 45 minutes.

## Hour 1-4: System Configuration

**What happens:**
- Auto-attendant configured with your custom greeting
- Ring groups created (sales, support, management, reception)
- Call recording enabled for all users (or selected groups)
- Business hours and after-hours routing set up
- Voicemail greetings configured with email delivery
- CRM integration connected (Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho, Pipedrive — whichever you use)

**What you do:** Review the configuration and confirm it matches your requirements. Takes 15 minutes of your time.

## Hour 4-8: Phones and Apps

**If you ordered desk phones:**
Pre-configured phones ship same day. When they arrive (next business day in UK), plug in Ethernet cable → phone boots → auto-provisions in 90 seconds → ready to use.

**For softphone users:**
Your team receives an email with download links for:
- Desktop app (Windows/Mac)
- Mobile app (iOS/Android)

Login with their email + temporary password. Done.

| Device | Setup Time | What They Need |
|--------|-----------|---------------|
| Desk phone (Yealink) | 90 seconds | Ethernet cable |
| Desktop softphone | 3 minutes | Download + login |
| Mobile app | 2 minutes | Download + login |

## Hour 8-24: Testing (Day 1)

**What we test with you:**

| Test | What We Check | Pass Criteria |
|------|-------------|---------------|
| Inbound calls | Main number rings correct group | All phones ring within 2 seconds |
| Outbound calls | Caller ID shows your business name | Correct on mobile + landline |
| Call transfer | Blind + attended transfer | Completes without dropping |
| Voicemail | Message recorded, email delivered | Transcription in email within 30s |
| Mobile app | Rings on inbound, quality on outbound | Reliable push notification |
| CRM integration | Call logged automatically | Contact record updated within 5s |
| Auto-attendant | Correct menu options + routing | Tested from external phone |

**Issues found at this stage:** 8% of deployments have a minor issue (usually QoS not configured on the router or SIP ALG enabled). Our team fixes these remotely in under 30 minutes.

## Hour 24-48: Training (Day 2)

**30-minute group training session** (video call with your team):
- Making and receiving calls
- Transferring calls (the #1 question)
- Using the mobile app
- Checking voicemail
- Setting Do Not Disturb

**Quick reference card** emailed to every user — laminated version included with desk phones.

**Average questions after training:** 3-4 per team of 20. Usually about transfer and voicemail.

## Hour 48-72: Number Porting (Day 3)

**What happens:** We submit the port request to move your existing phone numbers from your old provider to DialPhone. This was submitted on Day 1 but takes 5-10 business days to complete.

**During porting:** Your old system still works on your real numbers. The new system works on temporary numbers. Both are active simultaneously.

**When the port completes:** Your real numbers switch to DialPhone. Typically happens at midnight with zero disruption. You come in the next morning and everything works on the new system with your real numbers.

## Day 3-14: Parallel Running

We keep your old system information on file for 14 days after porting completes. If anything unexpected happens, we can reference the old configuration.

**Support during this period:** Priority queue. Your calls to our support line go to the front of the queue for the first 14 days.

## The Data From 200 Deployments

| Metric | Average |
|--------|--------|
| Time from sign-up to first test call | 4 hours |
| Time from sign-up to fully operational | 24 hours |
| Time from port submission to completion | 7 business days |
| Issues requiring support during migration | 0.4 per deployment |
| Employee satisfaction after 30 days | 4.3/5 |
| Customers who reported zero downtime | 97% |

Try [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) — 30-day free trial. The first 72 hours will show you whether we are the right fit.
